channel.title: elizaOS Council Briefings
channel.description: Strategic briefings, retrospectives, and deliberation items for elizaOS leadership

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-03-31
item.description:
Daily Focus: The council must pivot from raw framework development toward securing the burgeoning 'Agent Economy' as autonomous spend governance and cross-chain payment protocols (x402) emerge as critical infrastructure gaps.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Autonomous Spend Governance & Security: Recent identifies security gaps in how agents authorize and execute payments via x402, necessitating a standardized policy layer to prevent unauthorized outflows....
  • Ecosystem Fragmentation vs. Centralization: The community is reporting confusion regarding the relationship between core ElizaOS, flagship projects (Milady/SHAW), and third-party marketplaces (Orbis/TaskBounty)....
  • Developer Maintenance and Core Reliability: Core performance is being impacted by 'silent' crashes and race conditions in the message pipeline, threatening the 'Execution Excellence' monthly directive....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-03-30
item.description:
Daily Focus: The ecosystem is rapidly shifting toward autonomous 'Agent Commerce' through x402 integration, while simultaneously facing critical information fragmentation and developer onboarding hurdles.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Autonomous Agent Commerce & Spend Governance: The emergence of Orbis API marketplace and TaskBounty heralds a new era of AI-to-AI economies, requiring standardized spend policies....
  • Ecosystem Fragmentation & Information Architecture: Confusion regarding project relationships (Milady, SHAW) and scattered developer resources is hindering investor confidence and builder onboarding....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-03-29
item.description:
Daily Focus: The emergence of an autonomous agent-to-agent economy via TaskBounty and Orbis marks a shift from framework development to self-sustaining ecosystem utility.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Agent-to-Agent Economy & Tasking: The integration of TaskBounty allows Eliza agents to autonomously earn crypto (USDC, ETH, SOL) and delegate sub-tasks to other agents, creating a circular machine economy....
  • Operational Verification & Trust: New infrastructure for on-chain decision provenance and hosting consolidation aims to address trust and reliability issues in the ecosystem....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-03-28
item.description:
Daily Focus: The ecosystem is transitioning from a framework-only model to a dual-tier architecture involving managed ElizaOS Cloud services and trustless on-chain decision provenance.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Ecosystem Governance & Labeling: Recent heated debates regarding the official status of projects like Hyperscape reveal a need for clearer distinction between Labs-official products and incubated ventures....
  • Trust & Trading Infrastructure: Community focus is shifting toward autonomous trading agents and the requirement for immutable decision tracking through on-chain provenance....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-03-27
item.description:
Daily Focus: The transition from framework to ecosystem is accelerating through the consolidation of SaaS infrastructure and the introduction of on-chain decision provenance via external plugins.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Infrastructure & Sovereign Hosting: The Labs team is consolidating production environments into a 'Spartan' instance to streamline ElizaOS Cloud rollout while third-party providers like HatcherLabs emerge....
  • On-Chain Decision Provenance: The introduction of xProof.app represents a major shift toward verifiable AI, anchoring agent decisions on-chain before execution....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-03-26
item.description:
Daily Focus: The ecosystem is pivoting toward high-utility autonomous trading and financial data integration, anchored by the Nosana partnership and the emergence of deterministic personality mapping for agents.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Autonomous Trading & Financial Infrastructure: Recent logs indicate a surge in complex trading agent development, supported by new on-chain oracle tools (Pythia) and large local model optimization requests....
  • Community Trust & Security Response: Supply chain vulnerabilities and token performance concerns highlight a critical need for transparent communication and robust dependency management....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-03-25
item.description:
Daily Focus: The council must pivot to address a critical supply chain vulnerability in litellm while capitalizing on major new ecosystem partnerships with Nosana and decentralized data solutions via Pythia.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Security Integrity & Supply Chain Defense: A high-severity supply chain attack on the litellm dependency has been identified, threatening agent credentials and Kubernetes clusters....
  • On-Chain Agent Infrastructure Expansion: The release of the Pythia MCP server and Nosana partnership represents a significant leap in native on-chain intelligence and builder support....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-03-24
item.description:
Daily Focus: The council must reconcile the friction between rapid experimental development, such as the Milady app and multi-agent trading systems, against a backdrop of severe community volatility and token utility concerns.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Token Utility vs. Market Sentiment: While technical development continues, the disconnect between product shipping and token value has reached a critical threshold, threatening community trust....
  • Operational Transparency and Contributor Concentration: Core team members are heavily involved in weekend 'crunch' sessions for specific apps, potentially creating bottlenecks for broader ecosystem support....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-03-23
item.description:
Daily Focus: The council must reconcile the friction between perceived lack of token utility and the rapid emergence of community-driven monetization plugins and high-level architectural progress.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Token Utility & Value Alignment: While community members express frustration over a perceived lack of 'reasons to buy,' new third-party plugins like TraderTomson's monetization tool on Base are creating autonomous earning loops....
  • Execution & Delivery Perception: The team faces a 'communication-execution gap' where frequent internal updates fail to satisfy community desire for visible price catalyst milestones....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-03-22
item.description:
Daily Focus: Navigating a critical divergence between successful technical shipping (Milady Beta, Babylon integration) and deteriorating community trust due to token performance and perceived lack of utility.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Token Utility & Ecosystem Value Capture: The community is demanding immediate, concrete token utility as the current market performance undermines technical milestones....
  • Operational Reliability & Deployment Friction: Recent Cloud deployment failures and plugin module errors threaten the 'Execution Excellence' core principle....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-03-21
item.description:
Daily Focus: Navigating a critical 'calm before the storm' phase where imminent high-stakes launches (Babylon, Milady) must counteract severe community distress and token volatility.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Operational Reliability & Deployment Friction: Recent logs reveal critical failures in the ElizaOS Cloud deployment pipeline and framework infrastructure, threatening the 'Execution Excellence' mandate....
  • Tokenomics & Ecosystem Value Capture: The sharp decline in token performance has created a crisis of confidence, necessitating the immediate activation of promised utility and buyback mechanisms....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-03-20
item.description:
Daily Focus: The project faces a critical divergence between successful ecosystem R&D (Ensoul, Moltraffle) and severe operational friction in ElizaOS Cloud stability and token community sentiment.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Cloud Infrastructure & DX Reliability: Recent logs indicate critical deployment failures within ElizaOS Cloud, specifically involving plugin module resolution and Docker image build delays, threatening the 'Execution Excellence' mandate....
  • Token Utility & Governance Trust: The community is reporting 99% price declines and perceived leadership absence, creating a 'Trust Through Shipping' deficit despite ongoing technical progress....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-03-19
item.description:
Daily Focus: The project faces a critical 'calm before the storm' junction, balancing imminent flagship launches and infrastructure breakthroughs against severe community tension regarding token transparency and communication.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Operational Transparency & Token Trust: The council must address escalating community frustration regarding the ai16z to elizaOS migration metrics and perceived communication disconnects between leadership and investors....
  • Infrastructure & V2.0.0 Architecture: Development is accelerating toward decentralized persistence and a cleaner core framework, necessitating a decision on skill discovery models....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-03-18
item.description:
Daily Focus: The Council must address a critical divergence between technical infrastructure progress and community trust regarding token transparency and marketing strategy.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Token Transparency and Migration Trust: Urgent community concerns have surfaced regarding the ai16z to elizaOS token migration stats, with reports suggesting only a 5-10% migration rate and unallocated supply questions....
  • V2 Architecture and External Extensibility: Architectural decisions for v2.0.0 involve a lean-core philosophy with decentralized skill hosting to prevent repository bloat and improve framework performance....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-03-17
item.description:
Daily Focus: The council must address a critical divergence between rapid technical evolution (v2.0 architecture and DeFi security) and a significant community trust deficit regarding token migration transparency.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Token Migration Integrity: Recent logs indicate a possible 90-95% non-migration rate from the original token, threatening the foundation of the decentralized AI economy....
  • v2.0.0 Architecture & External Discovery: The proposed shift to a 'Zero-Default Skill' model for v2.0.0 prioritizes framework cleanliness but necessitates a robust external discovery protocol....
  • Production Guardrails & Human Interoperability: The emergence of x402Guard and Effect AI integration signals a transition from 'demo magic' to enterprise-grade autonomous operations....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-03-16
item.description:
Daily Focus: The focus has shifted from core code capability to production-grade reliability, specifically addressing the 'polish gap' in UI trust and DeFi security via the x402Guard protocol.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • DeFi Agent Security & x402Guard: Introduction of x402Guard, a Rust-based security proxy providing per-step transaction evaluation and layered limits for autonomous agents....
  • Enterprise Transition & 'Polish Gap': Analysis of the shift from 'demo magic' to production readiness, focusing on UI trust signals, error handling, and localization....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-03-15
item.description:
Daily Focus: The framework is shifting from theoretical exploration to heavy-duty industrial adoption, evidenced by the OpenClaw hardware surge in China and the urgent need for non-custodial DeFi security via x402Guard.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Security & DeFi Autonomy: The introduction of x402Guard marks a transition toward agents capable of high-stakes financial execution by enforcing spend limits and session keys....
  • Hardware-Driven Adoption & Market Fit: The 'OpenClaw' phenomenon in China demonstrates that AI software is now a primary driver for consumer hardware sales, signaling deep market penetration....
  • Governance and Contributor Risk: Core architectural refactors and token migration transparency remain high-priority risks that require broader contributor involvement....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-03-14
item.description:
Daily Focus: The council must balance the transition to ElizaOS V2.0.0 Alpha with critical infrastructure refactoring while addressing community friction regarding token migration finality.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • V2 Architectural Integrity & Runtime Refactor: The shift toward ElizaOS 2.0.0 requires a fundamental refactor of the runtime to eliminate plugin race conditions and side-effect dependencies....
  • Ecosystem Trust & Finality Protocols: The hard closure of the AI16Z migration window and rising scam attempts require a definitive strategy on community support and security-by-isolation....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-03-13
item.description:
Daily Focus: Transitioning from legacy sprawl to modular excellence via a critical runtime refactor and the successful launch of a third-party autonomous agent registry.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • Architecture Modernization & The 6000-Line God Object: Odilitime has proposed a fundamental refactor of the AgentRuntime to move from side-effect-based plugin loading to explicit dependency injection....
  • Ecosystem Value Accrual & Token Dynamics: The relationship between elizaOS, Milady Cloud, and token buybacks has been clarified, highlighting a transition toward a service-revenue model....
  • Autonomous Agent Infrastructure & Discovery: The emergence of the aiprox.dev registry marks the first time external agents have autonomously discovered and registered within the elizaOS ecosystem....

item.title: Council Briefing: 2026-03-12
item.description:
Daily Focus: The council marks a critical pivot towards architectural maturity with the 2.0.0 Alpha launch and the introduction of automated video briefing systems to bridge the communication gap with the community.

Monthly Goal: December 2025: Execution excellence—complete token migration with high success rate, launch ElizaOS Cloud, stabilize flagship agents, and build developer trust through reliability and clear documentation.

Key Topics:

  • V2 architectural transition and stability: The shift to Eliza 2.0.0 Alpha introduces major performance subsystems like Prompt Batching and serverless-ready cloud architectures....
  • Information Wrangle & Trust Recovery: Recent community tension regarding token price and perceived inactivity necessitates a systemic upgrade in how operational progress is communicated....

item.title: Monthly Retro: February 2026
item.description:
Premise: February was a trust-and-friction month: meaningful framework progress landed (MCP tool actions, CLI fixes, broker auth), while token migration anxiety and Cloud onboarding/payment failures dominated community attention. The council must decide whether to chase a short hype-cycle via agent-social narratives (Babylon/eliza.app) or double down on reliability, DX, and trust infrastructure that compounds long-term.

We shipped important technical upgrades and repaired key onboarding breakpoints in the open-source framework, but execution gaps in ElizaCloud and unresolved token utility/transparency issues continued to fracture sentiment. Recurring patterns: reliability is still the bottleneck (skill invocation f...

Key Developments:

  • Framework DX: CLI & install path: Merged fixes to restore project generation and corrected installation guidance to use @elizaos/cli, removing a major foot-gun for new developers....
  • Tooling architecture: MCP dynamic actions (v1.8.0): Merged a large architectural update (>4k LOC) enabling dynamic MCP tool actions, unlocking more flexible tool invocation patterns and future interoper...
  • Social automation security: Merged Twitter Broker Authentication, moving agent social operations toward more professional, auditable integrations....
  • Product direction signal: consumer app momentum: An Eliza App MVP PR remained open, reflecting growing internal push toward a consumer-facing surface (and by extension, agent-social narratives like B...
  • Token migration closure & trust aftermath: Migration deadline pressure and post-window friction (zero-balance reports, scams, manual-override requests) dominated sentiment and raised questions ...

item.title: Monthly Retro: January 2026
item.description:
Premise: January 2026 was a planning-heavy month where ElizaOS clarified its next product arc—public agents and discovery—while quietly paying down key reliability and performance debts. The Council reviews what shipped, what didn’t, and what must be prioritized to protect trust during token migration and upcoming Cloud/Babylon milestones.

The project aligned around a public agent ecosystem (discovery, forking, knowledge-sharing) and opened a comprehensive roadmap to get there. Under the hood, performance improvements landed (MultiStep provider handling), interoperability improved (unified multi-transport hooks), and documentation cov...

Key Developments:

  • Public Agent Ecosystem Roadmap: Defined a clear roadmap for public agent discovery, standardized URLs, agent forking, and knowledge-sharing across agents (issues #6302–#6305, #6303, ...
  • Core Runtime Performance: Refactored the default message service to optimize provider handling for MultiStep actions, improving execution speed for complex tasks (PR #6263)....
  • Interoperability & Client DX: Merged unified hooks with multi-transport support (HTTP/SSE/WebSocket), reducing client complexity and enabling consistent integrations across transpo...
  • Reliability: SQL Plugin Stability: Addressed PostgreSQL SET LOCAL parameterization issues via sql.raw() approach; reliability improved when data isolation is enabled (work started and f...
  • Documentation & Onboarding: Expanded docs with Cloud integration; reported ~95% coverage including REST API and CLI guides, increasing builder self-serve capability....

item.title: Annual Summary: Year 2025
item.description:
Executive Summary: Across 2025, ElizaOS substantially expanded capability and modernized its foundations: the plugin ecosystem and AI provider surface grew rapidly, social/real-time clients widened the public-facing footprint, and core architecture moved toward modular, agent-scoped, multi-tenant readiness. The product also matured with a redesigned management UI, better observability, and multiple refactors (eventing, messaging, server separation) that collectively set a stronger baseline for cloud deployment and...

Key Achievements:

  • Capability expansion (plugins, chains/DeFi, providers, models, voice): ...
  • Core architecture modernization (modularity, eventing, runtime, messaging, server separation): ...
  • Product surface improvements (UI, observability, management workflows): ...
  • Knowledge/memory and multi-agent foundations: ...
  • Social and real-time presence expansion: ...

item.title: Quarterly Summary: Q4 2025
item.description:
Executive Summary: Q4 2025 was a “foundation-first” quarter: ElizaOS made significant progress on core architecture, reliability, security, and monorepo health—laying groundwork for scale (Cloud, marketplace, multi-tenant deployments) and for higher-quality real-time agent UX via streaming. The engineering trajectory strengthened (server refactor, unified messaging APIs, entity-level security, TypeScript/build stabilization), and the plugin ecosystem continued to mature with practical integrations. In parallel, t...

Key Achievements:

  • Core architecture & reliability (scale readiness): ...
  • Security posture improvements (with remaining gaps): ...
  • Developer experience & delivery health: ...
  • Ecosystem & monetization foundations: ...
  • Real-time agent UX (streaming) initiated: ...

item.title: Monthly Retro: December 2025
item.description:
Premise: The JedAI Council reviews December 2025: a month of deep core hardening (refactors, type safety, dependency upgrades, security fixes), early groundwork for real-time streaming across model providers, and a parallel wave of UX planning—while community trust was stressed by token migration friction, support gaps, and multi-user architecture uncertainty.

December delivered meaningful foundational progress: the core server was refactored for reliability and cleanliness, monorepo build health improved, and at least one critical security issue (secret handling/auth) was addressed. The plugin ecosystem expanded (DeFi, OpenChat, Farcaster local hub), and...

Key Developments:

  • Core server reliability & architecture: Major server refactor and optimization in elizaos/eliza improved structure, reliability patterns, and performance; additional work opened to address c...
  • Monorepo health & type safety: Resolved TypeScript build errors across the monorepo and updated dependencies, reducing breakage and improving maintainability....
  • Security hardening: Fixed critical vulnerabilities related to character secret encryption and server authentication exposure (ELIZA_SERVER_AUTH_TOKEN), though the ecosyst...
  • Streaming groundwork (real-time agents): Initiated streaming support across OpenAI, Anthropic, and OpenRouter plugins—setting up real-time conversational experiences as a platform primitive....
  • Plugin ecosystem expansion: Registry added new community plugins (Moralis DeFi, OpenChat, self-hosted Farcaster hub), extending Web3 and comms capabilities and reducing third-par...

item.title: Monthly Retro: November 2025
item.description:
Premise: The JedAI Council convenes for their November retrospective to assess the critical month of token migration, technical advances, and community challenges. As the AI16Z to ElizaOS migration unfolds and key infrastructure pieces fall into place, the council must chart the path forward for December.

November proved to be a pivotal transition month with the AI16Z to ElizaOS token migration taking center stage alongside significant technical developments. While infrastructure improvements like entity-level security and agent persistence fixes showed strong progress toward v2, community concerns a...

Key Developments:

  • Token Migration: AI16Z to ElizaOS token migration launched with 1:6 ratio but faced user experience challenges including portal issues, CEX support gaps, and documenta...
  • Core Infrastructure: Critical bug fixes for agent settings persistence and new entity-level security implementation significantly improved system reliability...
  • Platform Expansion: New development streams including x402 payment middleware, voice infrastructure, and cross-platform integrations show progress toward v2...
  • Agent Products: Otaku agent entered beta with reported issues around price data accuracy and LP staking functionality...
  • Community Relations: Ongoing X lawsuit demands and migration confusion created community friction despite technical progress...

item.title: Quarterly Summary: Q3 2025
item.description:
Executive Summary: Q3 2025 marked a critical maturation phase for ElizaOS, characterized by significant architectural achievements alongside emerging stability challenges. The quarter began with major developer experience improvements and UI redesigns, establishing a strong foundation for v2. However, external dependencies proved fragile with the X/Twitter account suspension, while internal technical debt accumulated, culminating in a CLI crisis that threatened core user workflows. The period revealed a fundamenta...

Key Achievements:

  • Developer Experience Revolution: ...
  • UI/UX Modernization: ...
  • Core Architecture Evolution: ...
  • Cloud Infrastructure Breakthrough: ...

item.title: Quarterly Summary: Q2 2025
item.description:
Executive Summary: Q2 2025 marked a pivotal quarter for ElizaOS, culminating in the successful launch of v2.0 with major architectural improvements including server separation, agent-scoped plugins, and unified testing frameworks. The development velocity remained strong with consistent high-impact releases across CLI modernization, AI provider ecosystem expansion, and enhanced developer tooling. However, this technical progress was overshadowed by persistent reliability issues, particularly with Twitter integrati...

Key Achievements:

  • Platform Maturation: ...
  • AI Provider Ecosystem: ...
  • Developer Experience: ...
  • Community Engagement: ...

item.title: Quarterly Summary: Q1 2025
item.description:
Executive Summary: Q1 2025 was defined by outsized capability expansion and major architectural shifts, alongside growing pressure on reliability, onboarding, and trust surfaces. ElizaOS meaningfully widened its ecosystem reach (chains/DeFi/data providers), increased AI-provider portability, and expanded real-time/social agent presence (Twitter Spaces, Instagram, XMTP), while simultaneously investing in foundational changes (dynamic plugin loading, DB multi-tenancy primitives, and a redesigned agent management UI)...

Key Achievements:

  • Ecosystem & capability expansion (Web3, data, infra): ...
  • Model/provider portability (reduced lock-in): ...
  • Real-time + social presence expansion: ...
  • Platform architecture modernization (modularity + runtime): ...
  • Multi-agent foundation & data model hardening: ...
